original_hypothesis.title: Modifying Distributable Tokens Mid-Distribution Corrupts Payouts
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The `setDistributableERC20s` function can be called by the owner at any time, including when `LockedForDistribution` is true. If the owner changes the distributable tokens array while a distribution is partially complete, the `distribute()` function will use the new token addresses but the old `erc20EntitlementPerUnit` values. This causes incorrect payout amounts, distribution of wrong tokens, or out-of-bounds reverts that permanently lock the contract.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While requiring an admin mistake, the economic consequences are severe: misallocation of funds to the wrong users or permanent locking of the protocol.
preconditions: Owner calls setDistributableERC20s while LockedForDistribution is true

initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Wrong tokens distributed or contract permanently locked due to array out-of-bounds

economic_questions: What happens if the new token array is shorter than the old one?
What happens if the new token array is longer?
validation_requirements: Simulate changing the token array mid-distribution
Verify out-of-bounds revert and permanent lock state

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Total loss of protocol utility or misallocation of funds

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Loss of yield or permanent lock of tokens

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
